title: Spiraeanthemum idenburgense

The Cunoniaceae family is a group of flowering plants that are commonly known as the white currant family. This family is part of the larger order Oxalidales, which consists of flowering plants with distinct characteristics. The classification of Spiraeanthemum idenburgense reflects its evolutionary relationships with other plants within the Cunoniaceae family and beyond.

The plant is valued for its traditional medicinal uses, offering potential health benefits such as anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may aid in wound healing and the management of infections. The bioactive constituents of Spiraeanthemum idenburgense include flavonoids, alkaloids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties.
